items.hook: According to Herodotus, the inhabitants, Cissii, were a 'wild', free people, resembling the Persians in their manners.
items.body: Cissia was a very fertile district of Susiana in the Persian Empire, on the Choaspes. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: A Covenant with Death is a 1967 American legal drama film directed by Lamont Johnson, with a screenplay by Lawrence B.
items.body: Marcus and Saul Levitt based on the 1964 Book of the Month novel of the same title by Stephen Becker. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Alan Turing short story sheds new light on WW2 codebreaker
items.body: Prof Sarah Dillon says the short story, Pryce's Buoy, offers fresh insights into the codebreaker.

items.hook: Like other parts of the world where people of Indian origin are settled, the teachings of Swami Dayananda Saraswati, founder of the Arya Samaj, made their way to South Africa during the beginning of the twentieth century.
items.body: Arya Samaj is a Hindu reform movement in South Africa. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: We now know how a black hole can be flung across the universe
items.body: When researchers found evidence of a black hole flying out of its galaxy, nobody knew how that could happen. Now we have an explanation

items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

I just finished reading Wimmy Road Boyz by Sufiyaan Salam. I

items.hook: Emily Austin is a Canadian writer based in Ottawa, Ontario, whose debut novel Everyone in This Room Will Someday Be Dead was a shortlisted finalist for the 2022 Amazon.ca First Novel Award.
items.body: Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: It Comes is a 2018 Japanese supernatural horror film, based on a novel of the same name and directed by Tetsuya Nakashima.
items.body: It stars Junichi Okada, Haru Kuroki, Nana Komatsu, Takako Matsu, and Satoshi Tsumabuki. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: King and Queen emerge from Tardis to open Glasgow Commonwealth Games
items.body: The Royal couple drew gasps and cheers from the audience at the Hydro as they appeared on stage in Glasgow.

items.hook: The Exclusive: Beat the Devil's Tattoo is a 2015 South Korean black comedy crime thriller film directed by Roh Deok.
items.body: It was released on October 22, 2015. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Lucy Caldwell: ‘Even your text messages fizz after reading James Joyce’
items.body:
The author on the power of folktales, being inspired by Ulysses – and the book that every woman should read

My earliest reading memory
The summer my middle sister was born, my mum and I would walk to the shops

items.hook: There are only a few anti-ageing supplements that actually work
items.body: When it comes to supplements to stave off the effects of ageing, columnist Graham Lawton admits he is a hardened sceptic. But he finds some evidence for a few that can improve biological age as we grow older

items.hook: The Mother of Dartmoor is a 1916 British silent drama film directed by George Loane Tucker and starring Elisabeth Risdon, Bertram Burleigh and Enid Bell.
items.body: One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Most neurons seem to be jacks-of-all-trades, not specialists
items.body: We tend to think of the brain as being made up of highly specialised cells, but a study in mice suggests that most neurons are generalists
